edge_hypothesis: The supertrend flip is a validated high-quality trend signal (PF=1.264 at ADX 15 with 85 trades, PF=1.540 at ADX default with 18 trades). However, the tail-harvester concept (extreme R:R 5:1 to 7:1) has NEVER been tested with supertrend. The supertrend flip captures the exact moment of trend reversal — a structurally ideal entry point for wide TP targets because the new trend has maximum runway ahead. Testing three ADX thresholds (15, 20, 28) with extreme R:R to find the sweet spot between trade count and signal quality.
invalidation: If PF < 1.0 across all ADX thresholds with 20+ trades, the supertrend flip signal decays too fast for tail harvesting. Revert to moderate R:R approach (previous validated 2.0:4.0 range).

variants.name: supertrend_strong_trend_tail_7to1
variants.description: Strong trend tail harvester: ADX > 28 ensures only established trends are traded. Supertrend flip in strong trend = high conviction. 7:1 R:R captures the explosive continuation. Fewer signals but higher quality. Previous strong-trend result: PF=1.540 but only 18 trades — extreme R:R may help or hurt at this signal frequency.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (trend reversal signal)
ADX_14 > 28 (strong trend confirmed, skip transitional zone)
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (bearish trend reversal)
ADX_14 > 28 (strong trend confirmed, skip transitional zone)
exit_rules: TP at 7.0x ATR from entry (tail harvester target)
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR from entry
risk_rules: Stop: 2.0x ATR (validated minimum 1.5x, wider for trend riding)
ADX > 28 filters out toxic transitional regime (PF=0.595-0.919)

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip for trend reversal signal
ADX_14 > 28 strong trend filter, excludes toxic transitional zone

variants.name: supertrend_relaxed_tail_7to1
variants.description: Relaxed ADX tail harvester: ADX > 15 (validated relaxation that increased trades from 16 to 85 while preserving PF=1.264). Now combined with extreme 7:1 R:R. Tests whether the higher trade count from relaxed ADX paired with extreme R:R produces a better risk-adjusted result than either approach alone.
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (trend reversal signal)
ADX_14 > 15 (relaxed trend gate, validated at PF=1.264)
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (bearish trend reversal)
ADX_14 > 15 (relaxed trend gate, validated at PF=1.264)
exit_rules: TP at 7.0x ATR from entry (tail harvester target)
Stop loss at 2.0x ATR from entry
risk_rules: ADX 15 relaxation validated in prior cycle (trades 16→85, PF preserved)

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip for trend reversal signal
ADX_14 > 15 relaxed gate (validated PF=1.264 at 85 trades)

variants.name: supertrend_moderate_widestop_6to1
variants.description: Moderate ADX with wider stop and 6:1 R:R. Tests ADX > 20 (default) with 2.5x ATR stop (wider to avoid noise whipsaws that caused DD blowouts in prior supertrend tests with tight stops) and 6:1 R:R. The wider stop sacrifices some R:R efficiency for trade survival — prior tests showed stops < 1.5x cause DD blowouts (181%).
entry_long: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from negative to positive (trend reversal signal)
entry_short: SUPERTd_7_3.0 flips from positive to negative (bearish trend reversal)
exit_rules: Stop loss at 2.5x ATR from entry (wider to survive noise)
risk_rules: Stop: 2.5x ATR (wider — prior tests with < 1.5x caused 181% DD blowout)
Wider stop prioritizes trade survival over R:R efficiency

components.notes: SUPERTd_7_3.0 direction flip for trend reversal signal
ATR_14 for stop/TP scaling, wider stop to prevent DD blowouts
